# openwrt_network_rule6 (Data Source)

Manages an IPv6 policy routing rule. Rules select which routing table to use based on packet attributes.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) Name of the section. This name is only used when interacting with UCI directly.

### Read-Only

- `action` (String) Rule action when matched: blackhole (drop silently), prohibit (drop with EACCES), or unreachable (drop with ENETUNREACH).
- `dest` (String) Destination IPv6 subnet to match in CIDR notation (e.g. "2001:db8:1::/48").
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable this rule without removing it.
- `goto` (Number) Jump to the rule with the specified priority instead of performing an action.
- `in` (String) Incoming network interface to match.
- `invert` (Boolean) Invert the sense of the match.
- `lookup` (String) Routing table to use when this rule matches (e.g. "main", or a numeric table ID).
- `mark` (String) Firewall mark to match, optionally with a mask (e.g. "0x100" or "0x100/0x100").
- `out` (String) Outgoing network interface to match.
- `priority` (Number) Rule priority. Rules are evaluated in ascending order.
- `src` (String) Source IPv6 subnet to match in CIDR notation (e.g. "2001:db8::/32").
- `tos` (String) Type of Service (TOS) value to match (hexadecimal, e.g. "0x10").
